data.title: Globe Telecom tests Nokia’s network exposure platform to enhance security in financial services
data.description:
Philippines-based Globe Telecom is collaborating with Nokia (NYSE:NOK) to provide banks and other enterprises with enhanced security through the utilization of network Application Programming Interfaces, the companies said on Monday.

Globe Telecom, which already uses a host of other Nokia (NYSE:NOK) solutions including 5G RAN, is testing Nokia’s Network Exposure Platform in expanding and simplifying the number of APIs available to the operator and its enterprise partners to enable the creation of security-focused applications.

“With cyberattacks on banking services accelerating, it is crucial that we make available the latest network-powered technologies to our enterprise customers and help them safeguard against fraud,” said Joel Agustin, Globe’s Head of Service Planning and Engineering.

data.title: Prosus to acquire Just Eat Takeaway.com for €4.1B in all-cash deal
data.description:
• Prosus (OTCPK:PROSY) said on Monday that it plans to acquire food delivery major Just Eat Takeaway's entire issued share capital for €20.30 per share via an all-cash public offer on the Amsterdam exchange for €4.1 billion ($4.3 billion).

  • The all-cash offer represents a 49% premium to the 3-month VWAP and a 22% premium to its highest share price in three months. The stock closed at €12.43 on Friday.

  • Just Eat Takeaway.com, a leading food delivery platform in 17 international markets, connects 61 million customers with 356,000 local partners. With strong brand awareness, it generated €26.3 billion in GTV in 2024 and an adjusted EBITDA of €460 million.

  • The development to accelerate growth at Just Eat Takeaway.com, leveraging its industry experience. Furthermore, Prosus's current food delivery presence outside of Europe will be enhanced by the acquisition of Just Eat Takeaway.com.

  • The deal is subject to customary pre-offer and offer conditions, including obtaining regulatory approvals.

  • "Prosus, with a proven track record of profitable growth, will leverage its extensive food delivery portfolio and strong technical and investment capabilities to create value for customers, drivers, partners, and shareholders," said Fabricio Bloisi, Prosus’s CEO.

data.title: Alibaba to invest over $52 billion in AI and cloud infrastructure over next three years
data.description:
Chinese tech company Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) on Monday unveiled plans to invest at least 380 billion yuan ($52.44 billion) in its cloud computing and artificial intelligence infrastructure over the next three years.

The investment, which exceeds Alibaba’s total AI and cloud spending over the past decade, comes as global competition between the United States, China, and Europe heats up.

The e-commerce giant had said it had plans to invest in the sector while announcing its results. During its latest earnings call, Alibaba CEO Eddie Wu emphasized that cloud computing remains Alibaba’s clearest revenue driver in AI, with demand for AI hosting services surging.

Alibaba‘s Cloud Intelligence Group’s revenue—excluding revenue from Alibaba-consolidated subsidiaries—grew 11% year-over-year for the three months ended December 31.

Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) has been developing large language models, or LLMs, to compete with its Western and local rivals. Last month, the company unveiled its Qwen2.5-VL model, which it said fared better than Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI’s GPT-4o, Amazon (AMZN)-backed Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet and Google’s (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini 2.0 Flash in math, document analysis, video analysis and question-answering evaluations.

data.title: KKR acquires controlling stake in Healthcare Global for $400M
data.description:
• KKR (NYSE:KKR) has announced to acquire a controlling stake in Indian hospital operator HealthCare Global Enterprises for $400 million.
  • As part of the deal, KKR to acquire a stake of up to 54% in HealthCare Global from CVC Capital Partners at INR 445 per share, with potential ownership between 54%-77% following an open offer to public shareholders and assume sole control of its operations.
  • Founded in 1989, Bengaluru-based HealthCare Global is one of India's largest cancer-care providers, operating 25 medical-care centers.

  • This marks KKR's latest investment in India's healthcare sector, following investments in Baby Memorial Hospital, Healthium, Infinx, Max Healthcare, JB, and Gland Pharma.

  • With the development, Dr. BS Ajaikumar, HCG's founder, will transition to Non-Executive Chairman, focusing on clinical excellence and R&D.
  • The transaction, funded through KKR's Asia Fund IV is expected to close by the third quarter of 2025.

data.title: Asia markets mostly lower, mirroring Wall Street’s downturn; focus on Beijing's 2025 action plan
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets trade mostly lower on Monday following a downbeat session on Wall Street on Friday amid concerns over a slowing US economy, which could prompt Fed rate cuts. Focus shifts to China, as Beijing also recently unveiled its 2025 action plan, aimed at boosting foreign investment amid rising geopolitical tensions and ongoing economic uncertainties.

Caution grew ahead of crucial data from the US, including the PCE price indices and the second estimate of GDP growth, alongside speeches by several Fed officials.

Japan (NKY:IND) market closed. The Japanese yen traded around 149.3 per dollar on Monday, holding steady at 12-week highs as domestic markets were closed for a public holiday.

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.18% as mainland stocks paused after a three-week rally. The offshore yuan driven by strong investor sentiment following Beijing’s unveiling of its 2025 action plan. The plan seeks to attract foreign investment by easing access to China’s telecommunications and biotechnology sectors, highlighting a commitment to economic openness amid geopolitical tensions.

Additionally, China’s 2025 rural revitalization plan, focused on agricultural reforms and rural growth, strengthened the optimistic outlook.

On the monetary side, the PBOC kept its key lending rates in February 2025, reinforcing economic stability.

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 0.43% to 23,551 in early Monday trading, marking a second session of advance and reaching their highest level since early February 2022.

India (SENSEX) fell 1.05% to 74,595 in early deals on Monday, marking the fifth straight day of decline, tracking a fall in Asian peers.

Australia (AS51) rose 0.14% extending its losses for a sixth consecutive session. The Australian dollar gained 0.5% to around $0.638 on Monday, recovering losses from the previous session.

Domestically, investors are looking ahead to Australia’s monthly CPI data this week, which could provide guidance on the outlook for monetary policy.

In corporate news, Wisetech Global plunged more than 22% after the logistics software provider revealed that Chair Richard Dammery and three other directors would resign this week due to “intractable differences” over former CEO Richard White’s role.

In the U.S., on Friday, all three major indexes ended in red as economic data raised concerns about a slowing US economy and persistent inflation, prompting investors to seek safer assets. The slide followed Walmart's disappointing outlook and broader fears about the impact of President Trump's tariff policies.

U.S. stock futures gained on Monday as the market attempts to rebound from a sharp selloff last week: Dow +0.51%; S&P 500 +0.56%; Nasdaq +0.56%.

Key earnings from major retailers Home Depot and Lowe’s will also offer further insight into US consumer spending. Additionally, artificial intelligence leader Nvidia is set to report earnings on Wednesday, its first since the emergence of China's DeepSeek.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

Japan's CPI surpass expectations; annual inflation hits 2-year high to 4%

Japan reports larger-than-expected trade deficit for Jan, imports outpaces exports

Trump says 25% tariffs targeting cars, chips & pharma imports coming as soon as April 2

RBA cuts its cash rate by 25 bps for first time since 2020, in-line with market expectations

Japan’s economy expands by 0.7% Q/Q in Q4, beats estimates

data.title: Vistra raises dividend by 0.9% to $0.2235 a share
data.description:
• Vistra (NYSE:VST) declares $0.2235/share quarterly dividend, 0.9% increase from prior dividend of $0.2215.
  • Forward yield 0.59%
  • Payable March 31; to shareholders of record March 20; ex-div March 20.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 0.9% after paying a quarterly dividend of $0.2215 in the previous quarter.
  • See VST Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Saipem, Subsea7 to merge in $4.6B deal
data.description:
Saipem (OTCPK:SAPMF) (OTCPK:SAPMY) said Sunday it agreed to merge with rival oilfield services company Subsea7 (OTCPK:SUBCY) (OTCPK:ACGYF) in an all-stock deal valued at ~$4.63B.

According to the deal terms, Subsea7 (OTCPK:SUBCY) (OTCPK:ACGYF) shareholders will receive 6.688 Saipem (OTCPK:SAPMF) (OTCPK:SAPMY) shares for each Subsea7 share held and will distribute an extraordinary dividend for an amount equal to €450M immediately prior to completion.

The combined company, to be renamed Saipem7, will have a combined backlog of €43B (~$45B), revenue of ~€20B and EBITDA over €2B; Saipem (OTCPK:SAPMF) (OTCPK:SAPMY) and Subsea7 (OTCPK:SUBCY) (OTCPK:ACGYF) shareholders will own 50% each of the share capital of the combined company.

The companies said they expect the merger will achieve ~€300M in annual synergies in the third year after completion.

data.title: Shein's 2024 profit dropped nearly 40%: report
data.description:
Fast fashion retailer Shein’s profit dropped by a more than a third last year, ahead of the company’s planned initial public offering in London, the Financial Times reported Sunday, citing two people familiar with the matter.

According to the report, Shein’s 2024 profit sank nearly 40% to $1B, pressured by a poor final three months and competition from Temu (PDD), the sources said.

The people reportedly said sales rose 19% to $38B, and one said the numbers came from internal projections ahead of final results, the FT reported.

Bloomberg reported last week that Shein had been under pressure to cut its valuation to around $30B ahead of the IPO in the UK.

According to Bloomberg, shareholders suggested that the change was needed to get the IPO across the finish line.

data.title: Trump is mainly targeting China with metals tariffs, former trade adviser says
data.description:
President Donald Trump’s tariffs on aluminum and steel imports are mostly driven by concerns that China is glutting global markets and hurting U.S. manufacturing, a former trade adviser to the White House said.

The president this month signed an executive order to place 25% levies on all imports of steel and aluminum from any source. He said the tariffs, due to take effect on March 12, were needed to support U.S. production and to create jobs.

The order is “a reflection of this worry that what the U.S. has done thus far has not been sufficient to stop overcapacity in these sectors,” Kate Kalutkiewicz, senior director for international trade policy at the National Economic Council during Trump’s first term, said to the Canadian Broadcasting Corp.

“They almost entirely relate to China, and China’s non-market policies and practices which have enabled this global glut,” she said.

data.title: Credit card delinquencies, net charge-offs hold relatively stable in January
data.description:
In the first month of 2025, credit card metrics held roughly stable, as the labor market remains solid and household balance sheets are generally still healthy. Lending activity slowed during the month, with loans falling 2.7% to $512B, the sum of eight banks, from $526.2B in the previous month.

On average, the credit card delinquency ticked up to 3.07% from 2.90% in December, while the net charge-off rate eased ever so slightly to 4.29% from 4.31% during the month. But the average was heavily influenced by a significant month-over-month improvement in Bread Financial Holding's (NYSE:BFH) delinquency and charge-off rates. Excluding Bread, the average delinquency rate rose to 2.63% from 2.40% in the prior month, and the average net charge-off rate climbed to 3.78% from 3.68%.

The data looked more encouraging when looking at pace of change. "All card names reported faster Y/Y declines in delinquency rates, with the average now down -25bp Y/Y vs prior -13bp Y/Y," Morgan Stanley analyst Jeffrey Adelson wrote in a note to clients.

He affirmed that the standout in January was Bread Financial (NYSE:BFH), which had the largest beat on net charge-offs vs. Morgan Stanley's estimate. "Losses & delinquencies both outperformed seasonality, with faster Y/Y declines," Adelson noted.

The underperformer was American Express (NYSE:AXP), he said, with consumer and small business net charge-offs falling short of Morgan Stanley estimates and January seasonality, and reaccelerating Y/Y.

However, loan growth was slower than he expected, at an average of ~3.3% Y/Y, led by Bread Financial (BFH), down 190 basis points to -2% Y/Y.
